payload.text: Design a website like Duolingo

**Defining the Requirements**

Okay, I'm solidifying the core requirements. I'm focusing on the Duolingo model: interactive exercises, gamified elements, structured learning paths, and progress tracking. I'm also planning to leverage the Gemini API for real-time feedback on user input. I am starting to consider the user interface (UI) to promote accessibility and enjoyment.


**Outlining Core Features**

I am now focusing on detailed feature outlines. The home/dashboard will display learning paths and goals. The lesson interface will utilize interactive exercises. Mock leaderboards, a store, and an AI tutor are planned. User profile integration is also included. I'm exploring a color scheme mimicking Duolingo. I've chosen React, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS for the frontend, with Lucide React icons, and Framer Motion for animation. I will be using Gemini 3 Flash for AI integration. I am organizing files based on naming conventions.


**Structuring the Project**

I'm now diving into project structure, making component-level decisions. I've broken it down into layouts, lesson paths, and exercise components. I'm focusing on defining the types, and constants. I'm setting up the core services, including Gemini integration to check answers, generate exercises, and explain concepts. I'm also planning the core state variables and creating the initial boilerplate. I'm considering the structure of the sidebar and unit cards.

entries.description: Add application metadata with microphone permissions for language practice.
Main HTML entry point with Tailwind CSS and Font configuration.
React entry point initializing the app.
Core type definitions for the learning application.
App constants and mock data.
Service for interacting with Gemini API to generate exercises and feedback.
Navigation sidebar consistent with Duolingo's UI.
Status bar with hearts, gems, and language selector.
Interactive lesson node component for the learning path.
The fullscreen interactive exercise view.
Main application component managing routing, state, and the learning path.

payload.text: Allow users to customize their profile avatars and display names in the profile section.

**Considering Profile Customization**

I'm currently focused on incorporating profile customization, specifically avatar and display name options, into the "Linguo" app. I've noted the existing `UserStats` state in `App.tsx` but will need to expand on it with new user profile state. The hardcoded profile data currently displayed needs to be dynamic.


**Expanding User Profile Details**

I'm now extending the `UserStats` type in `types.ts` to include `displayName` and `avatarUrl`. I'll update the initial state in `App.tsx` accordingly and create a new state variable for profile editing. This will involve adding an "Edit Profile" button to the profile section's UI. When editing is active, I'll show input fields for the `displayName` and `avatarUrl`. I plan to offer a pre-defined set of avatar options using a service like `dicebear` to simplify image handling.

entries.description: Extend UserStats to include displayName and avatarUrl for profile customization.
Update the profile tab to allow users to edit their display name and choose from a selection of avatars.

payload.text: In the ExerciseScreen, add a 'skip' button that allows the user to skip the current exercise or lesson, perhaps costing gems or impacting streak. This should be a quality-of-life feature.

**Implementing Skip Functionality**

I'm focusing on adding a "Skip" button to the `ExerciseScreen`. My priority is enabling users to skip exercises. I plan to introduce a gem cost as a consequence for using the skip feature. This aligns well with the gamified design.


**Refining Skip Behavior**

I'm now refining the behavior of the skip functionality. Skipping will cost gems (let's say 5), and move to the next exercise. I'm leaning toward not counting the skipped exercise as "correct" for lesson progress, mirroring the missed exercise concept. This means the user could repeat it at the end, just like a missed exercise. I'm updating `ExerciseScreen` and `ExerciseScreenProps` and making the button conditional based on whether the question has been answered or not.


**Designing Skip UI and Logic**

I'm now integrating the skip feature more fully into the UI and logic. I'm focusing on UI placements like the footer of the `ExerciseScreen` for the skip button, next to the "Check" button only when the answer hasn't been submitted yet. The `ExerciseScreen` will internally handle the skip action by calling an external `onSkip` prop and then the internal `handleNext`. The `onSkip` will likely return a boolean indicating success (enough gems).

entries.description: Add a skip button to the exercise screen that allows users to move to the next exercise by spending gems.
Implement the handleSkipExercise logic in the App component to manage gem deduction and allow skipping.

payload.text: The website opening came on the same login page

**Designing a Landing Page**

I'm currently focused on designing a landing page that's similar to the provided Duolingo example. The initial thought is to introduce a welcome screen before the app's main dashboard. This landing page will feature a prominent logo and a user-friendly language selection dropdown. My objective is to create a smooth entry point for users.


**Visualizing the Hero Section**

Now, I'm concentrating on the visual design of the hero section. I'm thinking of a central illustration featuring the characters and Duo. On the right, I'll have a headline: "The free, fun, and effective way to learn a language!". I'll include two primary buttons: "GET STARTED" and "I ALREADY HAVE AN ACCOUNT". I'll also add a footer with a display of flags and language names to complete the landing page.

entries.description: Create a new LandingPage component that mimics the Duolingo login screen from the user's screenshot.

payload.text: add other language studdying options

**Expanding Language Options**

I'm currently examining the Linguo app's codebase. I've pinpointed the `types.ts` file, where languages are defined, and noticed the limited options. I've also identified `App.tsx` and the default language setting. I need to figure out how to best integrate more language choices.


**Adding Language Selections**

I'm now focusing on implementing the language selection. The `TopBar.tsx` displays flags which need to be generalized, and the `LandingPage.tsx` needs a language picker. Also, I'll update the `types.ts` file to include additional languages such as Chinese, Korean, Arabic, and more. Then I will ensure the `geminiService.ts` is ready to handle these new languages.

payload.text: I ALREADY HAVE AN ACCOUNT this button clicking time go to this page

**Designing Login Screen**

I'm focusing on the "Log In" screen design. Specifically, I'm noting that the existing landing page design includes an "I ALREADY HAVE AN ACCOUNT" button. Clicking this should launch the login screen. Based on the screenshot, this screen appears to use a dark background, similar to a dark theme. It also has a close (X) button in the top-left corner.


**Defining Login Screen Elements**

Okay, I've got a much clearer picture for the login screen. It needs a "SIGN UP" button in the top right, a "Log in" title, and fields for email/username and password. I'm going to include a "FORGOT?" link. Plus, a big light blue "LOG IN" button, an "OR" divider, and social login buttons for Google and Facebook. Lastly, I'll put legal text at the bottom. I am planning to create `LoginPage.tsx` now.

payload.text: In the App.tsx file, modify the handleLessonFinish function to check if all exercises in the active lesson were completed successfully. If so, award bonus gems or XP.

**Refining the Logic**

I'm now zeroing in on the `handleLessonFinish` function. The initial implementation is too simplistic. My focus is expanding the conditional logic within the function to account for *successful completion* of all exercises in the current lesson. I need to reward a gem bonus or some other incentive if the exercises are all perfect, above and beyond the baseline XP and gems.


**Evaluating Completion Accuracy**

I'm now thinking about how to get accuracy data from the `ExerciseScreen` component back to the `App` component. The current `onClose` callback only indicates completion, not perfection. I need to know if the user made any errors during the exercises. It looks like the simplest change is to add a parameter to the existing `onClose` callback function in `ExerciseScreen`. I can then check for accuracy in `App`.


**Clarifying Lesson Success**

I'm now determining how to accurately identify a "perfect lesson" completion. My focus has shifted to the `ExerciseScreen`'s heart-loss functionality. It seems like the simplest method is to track the number of times `onLoseHeart` is called, which should correlate with mistakes. The aim is to create a conditional check for a perfect lesson within `handleLessonFinish`. The logic is to adjust the reward for a perfect lesson completion.


**Implementing the Update**

I'm now implementing the changes to accurately reflect lesson perfection. My focus is on modifying the components for this update. I've updated `ExerciseScreen.tsx` to include `mistakeCount`, and passing `mistakeCount === 0` (isPerfect) to `onClose`. Now, I'm updating `App.tsx`'s `handleLessonFinish` signature to accept the `isPerfect` boolean and award bonus gems and XP if true. I am ready to test my implementation.

entries.description: Track mistakes and skips to determine if a lesson was "perfect", passing this information to the onClose callback.
Update handleLessonFinish to award bonus XP and Gems for "Perfect Lessons" (no mistakes or skips).

payload.text: Add functionality to track and celebrate unit and lesson completion milestones. Award a small gem bonus or unique badge for completing a unit. Update the UI to reflect these achievements.

**Considering Unit Completions**

I'm focusing on adding milestone tracking for unit and lesson completions. My current priority is awarding a gem bonus or badge upon unit completion. I'm reviewing the existing UI and `App.tsx` code to determine the best approach for integrating achievement displays and state updates.


**Refining Completion Logic**

I'm now refining the unit completion check, ensuring it correctly identifies when all lessons within a unit are marked as 'completed.' I'm going to update `App.tsx` to integrate this logic after `handleLessonFinish` and will trigger a milestone celebration if a unit is completed for the first time. I'm also planning to create a `MilestoneModal.tsx` for displaying achievements.

entries.description: Add badges to UserStats to track achievements.
Create a celebration modal for milestones like unit completion or perfect lessons.
Enhance handleLessonFinish to track unit completion milestones, award badges, and show celebration modal.

payload.text:
**Addressing Missing Variables**

I'm currently focused on resolving the "cannot find name" errors within `App.tsx`. Specifically, I'm identifying instances of undefined variables, `tempName`, `tempAvatar`, and `avatarName`, which are appearing on lines 192, 193, 199, 200, 344, 346, 359, and 360. My next step will be to examine the scope and declaration of these variables to ensure they're properly defined before their usage.


**Resolving Missing Declarations**

I've addressed the "cannot find name" errors in `App.tsx` related to `isEditingProfile` and the other variables. I imported `Trophy` from `lucide-react`, and now, I've declared state variables for `isEditingProfile`, `tempName`, and `tempAvatar` to resolve the undefined variable errors. I've also initialized `tempName` and `tempAvatar` with values from `stats`. I believe these changes will resolve all identified issues.


**Synchronizing Variable States**

I'm now focusing on synchronizing `tempName` and `tempAvatar` with the profile's state. I realized they need updating when `isEditingProfile` transitions. Currently, the code uses `tempName` and `tempAvatar` in `saveProfile` and resets them in `cancelProfileEdit`. I've decided to initialize them with values from `stats`. I'm reviewing the file to locate the correct insertion point for these new states, specifically after the definition of `stats` (Line 64), and confirming Trophy is being used and imported where it's needed.


**Refining State Synchronization**

I've confirmed the `Trophy` import is now present. I realized initializing `tempName` with `stats.displayName` initially could lead to stale values as `stats` updates during login. Therefore, I'm now focusing on synchronizing `tempName` and `tempAvatar` with `stats` *when* entering edit mode. I'm updating `tempName` and `tempAvatar` when `setIsEditingProfile(true)` is called, to ensure up-to-date profile data. I reviewed the existing logic within `handleStartLogin`, `saveProfile`, and `cancelProfileEdit`.


**Implementing State Updates**

I'm now implementing the state update. I realized I should introduce a new function, `startEditing`, to set the temporary variables *before* setting the edit mode state. This will ensure they reflect the current profile information. I'm adding `startEditing` to the code and calling it within the `onClick` handler of the edit button. The new states are initialized with `stats` data, and it's all in the `App.tsx` file. I've also verified that the `GoogleGenAI` library is utilized correctly in `geminiService.ts`.


**Identifying Remaining Errors**

Now I'm reviewing the provided error list to ensure all instances are addressed and resolved. I am verifying that the changes I've implemented have eliminated the "cannot find name" errors. I see that the reported errors on lines 192, 193, 195, 199, 200, 201, 235, 288, 293, 302, and 344 are related to previously un-declared states and imports, which I have addressed. I must check these thoroughly.


**Verifying Import Corrections**

I'm now verifying my recent changes within `App.tsx` and focusing on the identified error on line 360, "Cannot find name 'setTempName'". I am also reviewing the errors identified on lines 346 and 359, which reference `tempAvatar` and `tempName`, respectively, to confirm that these issues have been resolved by adding states and the import statements, particularly `Trophy` from `lucide-react`. The `tempAvatar === url` check in the profile edit section still looks correct.


**Confirming Code Integration**

I'm now integrating the changes related to the badge system and verifying their functionality within the application. I have addressed the recent issues involving undefined variables within the modal. I am verifying that the changes related to badge, userStats, unit, lesson and language types within `types.ts` are integrated correctly. I'm focusing on ensuring all defined badges in `ALL_BADGES` are implemented without conflicts.


**Finalizing Badge Integration**

I'm now focusing on integrating the badge and unit functionality. I'm ensuring the changes in `types.ts` correctly implement the badge and unit structures. The `ALL_BADGES` array is being carefully checked for consistency and adherence to the type definitions to ensure the application logic behaves as expected. The new state variables and imports seem to have resolved many issues in `App.tsx`.


**Refining Error Resolution**

I'm now focusing on a new issue: the error "Gemini failed to..." that's popping up when generating exercises, so I'm investigating `generateExercises` and the `Gemini` integration in `geminiService.ts`. I am stepping through the `handleStartLesson` function, specifically the `try...catch` block, to identify the source of the exception. I'm checking the `exercises` returned and the function calls. I will focus on making the program more robust to handle the `Gemini` failures in a more graceful manner, with better error handling.



**Improving Error Handling**

I'm now focusing on improving the error handling within the `handleStartLesson` function to manage the "Gemini failed to generate exercises" errors in a more robust manner. I'm carefully reviewing the `try...catch` block to pinpoint where the exception is occurring. I need to make the program more robust against these failures, by adding better error handling. I'm focusing on providing more descriptive error messages and potentially implementing retry logic to enhance reliability.


**Enhancing Unit Completion Logic**

I'm now refining the logic for unit completion. I've updated how lessons within a unit are marked as 'completed' or 'available'. The updated code checks if the entire unit was newly completed. If so, and the corresponding unit badge wasn't already earned, I'm adding it to `newBadges`. I'm also updating the `milestone` state to reflect the unit completion. This will ensure that badges are awarded correctly and milestones are triggered only upon successful unit completion.


**Optimizing Unit Completion Logic**

I'm now implementing the update I had planned for the unit-completion flow. I'm focusing on ensuring accurate badge and milestone triggers. I'm ensuring badges and milestones are only awarded upon successful unit completion, which is a key update. I am updating `setStats` now to incorporate added `xp` and `gems` from unit completion. I'm carefully reviewing the logic to prevent any unintended side effects.


**Refining Profile Updates**

I'm now refining the `saveProfile` and `cancelProfileEdit` functions. I've removed the `tempName` and `tempAvatar` initialization from `cancelProfileEdit`, now it's only updating the states. I'm focusing on ensuring data consistency when saving or canceling profile edits, to keep these in line with the stats in the background. I'm carefully reviewing these functions to ensure they properly update the state of the user profile, whether saved or abandoned.


**Analyzing Unit Completion Display**

I'm now analyzing the unit display logic, specifically the conditional rendering of the `Trophy` icon. I'm reviewing how `unit.lessons.every(l => l.status === 'completed')` determines whether the trophy appears. I'm verifying the logic and ensuring the trophy is displayed only when *all* lessons within a unit are marked as completed. I'm also confirming the visual attributes.

I'm checking that the lesson `status` is used. I am confirming all lessons are complete before the trophy shows.


**Verifying Unit Completion Display**

I'm now focused on the leaderboard state rendering. I'm verifying the current display for the leaderboard's conditional image and text. I'm reviewing if the leaderboard unlocks after the requisite number of lessons are completed, and ensuring that there are no logical flaws or gaps. I'm checking the logic that determines if the user has completed enough lessons to access the leaderboard.



**Refining Health Logic**

I'm now focusing on refining the health logic within the profile section, as this is critical to the UX. I'm verifying the conditional logic for displaying the "Get back to full health" button. I am confirming the gem expenditure for heart restoration. I'm also ensuring the code correctly deducts gems upon a successful heart replenishment.


**Refactoring Profile Editing**

I'm now refactoring the profile editing components to streamline their functionality and visual appeal. I've focused on ensuring the `setIsEditingProfile` state variable is handled correctly. I've now introduced the `Camera` import. I've also incorporated the `Pencil` icon import. My goal is a more intuitive and visually cohesive editing experience.


**Analyzing Badge Display**

I'm now examining how `ALL_BADGES` and `stats.badges` interact within the profile section's badge display, particularly within the provided code. I'm verifying the conditional rendering of badges based on `earned` status. I'm focusing on ensuring the visual cues (`border-[#ffc800]`, `bg-[#fffcf0]`, etc.) accurately reflect earned versus unearned badges, as well as the transition effects.


**Validating Lesson Completion**

I'm now focused on the lessons themselves, and their statuses. I'm verifying the completion status of the lessons. I am confirming that the unit's `Trophy` visibility correctly reflects complete lesson status. I am ensuring that the lesson state updates correctly.

I am checking that the logic used to determine if a lesson has been completed is valid. I'm going through the component, from top to bottom, to verify that there are no gaps.


**Refining Cancel Edit Function**

I'm now making refinements to `cancelProfileEdit` in the profile section, streamlining the functionality and state management. I've focused on ensuring `tempName` and `tempAvatar` reset states. I'm making sure that the profile can be properly canceled and the data stays consistent.


**Finalizing UI Refinements**

I'm now focusing on the profile section, specifically the visual representation of the stats and their associated styling. I am looking at how the "Day Streak" is presented, and ensuring consistency. I'm focusing on the integration of visual elements within the profile.

entries.description: Import Trophy icon from lucide-react and add missing state variables for profile editing functionality (isEditingProfile, tempName, tempAvatar).

payload.text: Add a new 'practice' tab to the sidebar that allows users to review previously completed lessons or focus on specific grammar/vocabulary areas without the pressure of losing hearts or impacting streaks.

**Adding Practice Tab**

I've outlined the necessary updates to integrate a "Practice" tab into Linguo. Specifically, I'll modify `Sidebar.tsx` to include the tab icon and label, and I plan to update `App.tsx` to manage its state and render the corresponding Practice view. This seems straightforward, and I can start implementing these changes now.


**Refining Specification for Practice**

I've specified further details for the practice mode, focusing on user interaction and visual elements. The "Practice" tab in the sidebar will be between "EXPLORE" and "LEADERBOARD", using a Dumbbell icon. The view will present card-based practice options that are consistent with the Linguo design, and will include different practice modes and XP rewards. Correct answers will award XP.

entries.description: Add the 'Practice' tab to the sidebar menu items with a dumbbell icon.
Add an 'isPractice' prop to disable heart loss and hide hearts UI during practice sessions.
Implement the Practice tab logic, including starting practice sessions and handling practice completion rewards.

**Expanding Language Support**

I'm currently focused on expanding the language support within the app. I'm starting by updating `types.ts` to fully incorporate the new languages into the `Language` union type. Subsequently, I'll revise `constants.tsx` to ensure all these added languages are accurately reflected within the `SUPPORTED_LANGUAGES` constant.


**Adding New Languages**

I've completed adding the requested languages to `constants.tsx`, and updated the `SUPPORTED_LANGUAGES` array with corresponding flag codes. Next, I'm reviewing UI components, such as `LandingPage`, to ensure they can handle the expanded language set, particularly the language selection modal. The addition of constructed languages poses a flag challenge; I'll explore suitable placeholders.


**Refining the Language List**

I'm now revising the language code list, considering `flagcdn`'s limitations with sub-region codes. I'll stick to 2-letter ISO country codes where possible, using 'un' for constructed and international languages like Klingon and Esperanto. I'll need to handle potential rendering issues for Esperanto's unique flag, so I'll explore fallback options in the UI. I also plan to recheck all existing mappings for consistency.


**Verifying Language Codes**

I've checked the grid layout used by `LandingPage` and verified it's functional with the added languages. I updated `types.ts` and `constants.tsx` to include the language additions. I've also double-checked flag codes, and determined that 'un' will be used for constructed languages like High Valyrian and Klingon, and 'gb' for Scottish Gaelic and Welsh. I've re-examined and updated the mappings for language flags and codes to align with current standards and usability, ensuring consistency across the codebase.

entries.description: Expand the Language union type to include all newly requested languages.
Update the SUPPORTED_LANGUAGES array with all requested languages and their corresponding flag codes for FlagCDN.

payload.text: In the 'learn' tab, for each unit, visually indicate the completion progress of its lessons. For example, show a progress bar or a count of completed lessons.

**Visualizing Lesson Progress**

I'm focusing on enhancing the "learn" tab by visually representing lesson completion within each unit. My current approach involves integrating either progress bars or a simple completed lesson count. I'm examining the `App` component to understand the existing structure and identify the best place to introduce this progress indicator.


**Implementing Progress Visuals**

I've zeroed in on the `App.tsx` file and am now focusing on the `units.map` section within the "learn" tab. My goal is to calculate completed lessons per unit and insert either a progress bar or text indicator, or ideally both. I'm exploring an approach like Duolingo's visual cues for a clean and intuitive design inside the unit header, to show completion alongside the title.


**Refining UI Progress**

I'm now implementing the progress bar design within the unit component. I've added a progress display to show the completed lessons versus the total, alongside a percentage. This structure will include a completed lessons count in the unit title, which is going to be displayed with a badge. I'm exploring the look of the trophy, to be displayed at the end of the lesson unit when completed.

entries.description: Add a progress bar and lesson count indicator to each unit header in the 'learn' tab to visually track completion status.
